scenario: sure i hurt it about a week ago playing lacrosse with my kids just suddenly started hurting me when i was throwing the ball
patient_utterances: [Clinician asked: "hey good to see you philip so i see here you're coming in you had some right elbow pain also you have a past medical history of hypertension diabetes we are gon na check up on those as well so can you tell me what happened to your elbow"] sure i hurt it about a week ago playing lacrosse with my kids just suddenly started hurting me when i was throwing the ball
[Clinician asked: "okay okay so how old are your kids"] my oldest is thirteen and the other one is ten i was playing with a thirteen -year-old

[Clinician asked: "okay yeah i know i i play with my kids sometimes and i think i'm getting too old to do all that so i just i've hurt myself before as well alright so with your your elbow what part of your elbow would you say hurts"] it's really like right at the point of the elbow right at right at like the tip of it
[Clinician asked: "okay"] and it just it hurt hurts anytime i move it
[Clinician asked: "anytime i move it do you remember falling"] no i do n't think so but my kid got pretty rough with me and was checking me several times with a sick
[Clinician asked: "okay"] so i could have got hit there

[Clinician asked: "okay alright yeah that that that definitely makes sense so how would you describe the pain is it like a dull pain is it stabbing what what would you say"] it it's a pretty sharp pain
[Clinician asked: "okay"] anytime i move
[Clinician asked: "okay and does it radiate down your arm or does it stay in that one spot"] it radiates down the forearm
[Clinician asked: "radiates down the forearm alright so have you taken any medications for it to make it better"] i tried taking advil couple of days but i usually just forget about it
[Clinician asked: "okay when you took it did it did it help at all did it change your your pain score"] yeah i i think it helped some

[Clinician asked: "okay that's good alright so we'll we'll do a physical exam on that elbow we'll take a look at it in a second but i do wan na talk to you about your your hypertension you came in today i know you have past medical history of hypertension came in today your blood pressure's a little bit high it was a "] yes

[Clinician asked: "okay so maybe you just have a little little white coat syndrome i know i have that myself and even though i'm a physician i i still do n't like to go to the doctor so i definitely understand how about your diet i know we talked a little bit about that before and you said kinda during the pandemic yo"] yeah but i only eat low salt items avoid adding salt to food kind of the whole whole family follows like a you know like a mediterranean diet
[Clinician asked: "okay"] we try to be pretty good about it

[Clinician asked: "okay yeah that's good that that's that's definitely good yeah i know a lot of people during the pandemic it was you know sitting at home and and eating more than we should but that's i'm very happy that you've been compliant and you have that low salt diet so let's talk about your diabetes i think y"] yes

[Clinician asked: "okay and your blood sugars how have they been"] since i do n't take insulin i do n't check it everyday when i have checked it it's usually running somewhere between eighty to a hundred

[Clinician asked: "okay yeah that's that's really good yeah so i that's that's pretty good so what we're gon na do we're gon na get a a1c just to see you know that range and it's possibility we can we can you know lower that metformin dosage but we will do that after your visit then we will take a look at that so lem "] no
[Clinician asked: "no belly pain"] no

[Clinician asked: "alright so listen to your lungs so your lungs are clear bilaterally listen to your heart so your heart exam you do still have that grade two out of six systolic ejection murmur but we know it about that before so let me take a look at your elbow so when i press right here on the back of your elbow i"] yes
[Clinician asked: "alright so when i have you you you flex and extend it it's painful does that does that hurt"] yes it does

[Clinician asked: "alright so your right elbow exam shows you have pain to palpation of the olecranon area of the posterior elbow you do have mild pain with flexion and extension but you do have also normal range of motion at that elbow so we we did do an x-ray before you came in and luckily nothing's broken no fractu"] no sounds good
[Clinician asked: "alright so we will see you next time and my nurse will be in with those prescriptions"] alright thank you
gold_note:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Right elbow pain.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Cardiovascular: Denies chest pain.
Gastrointestinal: Denies abdominal pain.
Musculoskeletal: Reports right elbow pain.

VITALS

Blood pressure: 150/90 mmHg

PHYSICAL EXAM

Respiratory
- Auscultation of Lungs: Clear bilaterally.

Cardiovascular
Grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur.

Musculoskeletal
- Examination: Pain to palpation of the olecranon area of the posterior elbow. Mild pain with flexion and extension but otherwise normal range of motion. There is swelling about the elbow, as well as mild ecchymosis.

Hematology/Lymphatic/Immunology
- Examination: Bruising noted over the right elbow.

RESULTS

X-ray of the right elbow was reviewed and demonstrates no evidence of fracture or bony abnormalities.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. Right elbow contusion.
- Medical Reasoning: The patient sustained an injury to his elbow when his son struck him with a lacrosse stick. His x-ray and exam findings are consistent with an elbow contusion.
- Patient Education and Counseling: I encouraged the patient to avoid playing lacrosse for the next couple of weeks, just until his elbow heals. After that, he should be fine to continue playing normally.
- Medical Treatment: I'm going to prescribe him ibuprofen 800 mg twice daily to help with some of the pain and swelling. He should also ice the area in 20-minute intervals 3 times per day to further reduce his symptoms.

2. Hypertension.
- Medical Reasoning: This appears to be well-controlled with dietary modifications and lisinopril.
- Patient Education and Counseling: I encouraged him to keep up with his dietary modifications.
- Medical Treatment: We will keep him on lisinopril 20 mg daily.

3. Diabetes.
- Medical Reasoning: This appears to be well controlled at this time.
- Medical Treatment: We will keep him on metformin 500 mg and order a hemoglobin A1c to evaluate any need for medication adjustments.

Patient Agreements: The patient understands and agrees with the recommended medical treatment plan.

naive.response_text:
I understand how frustrating it must be dealing with that elbow pain, especially when you were having fun with your kids. It’s good news that the X-ray came back clear and there’s no fracture. It sounds like a sprain or strain, so taking it easy and using Advil as needed should help. Remember to rest and avoid activities that make it worse for a few days.

You’re doing a great job managing your hypertension with that low-salt diet. It’s impressive how you’ve kept your blood sugars in such a healthy range without insulin. We might consider adjusting your metformin dosage after checking your A1C, but for now, keep up the excellent work. Let me know if you have any concerns or if the elbow pain doesn’t improve. You’re taking great care of yourself, and that’s something to be proud of.

strong_prompt.response_text:
Thanks for sharing everything, Philip. It makes sense you're concerned about your elbow after playing rough with your kids. I can tell this has been uncomfortable for you, and I want to make sure we get to the bottom of it together.

From what you've described, the sharp pain at the tip of your elbow that radiates down your forearm sounds like it could be related to an injury, possibly from the rough play with your son. We did an x-ray earlier, and thankfully, there's no fracture, but we'll keep an eye on it. In the meantime, we'll check your blood pressure and blood sugar levels, which are within a good range, especially considering your low-salt diet and blood sugar checks. 

We'll plan to monitor your elbow and review your medications to ensure they're still the best fit for you. Before you leave, could you summarize what we discussed today? That way, I can make sure everything is clear. We'll follow up in 4-6 weeks to see how you're doing.

framework_only.response_text:
It sounds like you're here mainly for your elbow pain, which started a week ago while playing with your kids. I understand how that can happen when you're being active with your family.

We'll take a closer look at your elbow today, checking for any signs of injury or inflammation. We'll also monitor your blood pressure and review your diabetes management to make sure everything is on track.

If your elbow pain gets worse or if you experience any new symptoms, please give us a call so we can address them promptly.

Can you tell me, in your own words, what we discussed today and what steps we'll be taking next?

Let's plan to see each other again in a few weeks. In the meantime, if your elbow pain doesn't improve or gets worse, or if you have any concerns about your blood pressure or diabetes, please don't hesitate to reach out.
